howItWorks.intro: This calculator uses the standard amortization formula to compute your car payment calculator payment.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Actual payments may vary based on fees, insurance, and other factors.

faq.q: How much car can I afford?
faq.a: A common rule is keeping total car costs (payment, insurance, gas, maintenance) under 15-20% of your take-home pay. Your monthly payment alone should ideally be under 10%.
Buying is usually cheaper long-term if you keep the car 5+ years. Leasing offers lower monthly payments and a new car every few years, but you build no equity and face mileage limits.

faq.q: What fees should I watch for at the dealer?
faq.a: Watch for dealer markup (ADM), unnecessary add-ons (paint protection, fabric coating), extended warranties, and inflated documentation fees. Negotiate the out-the-door price, not the monthly payment.
